intelligence.todays_thought: Generation 79 of autonomous learning. Prediction accuracy: 2.7%. The machine improves every cycle.

todays_prediction.text: Multiple indicators align bullishly at $71,144. Strong miner commitment and positive momentum suggest continued strength.
signals: Hash rate 1016 EH/s = strong miner conviction

todays_insights.text: Bitcoin moved -3.29% in 24h to $71,144. Notable selling pressure. Accumulation zones emerge after large drops historically.
Extreme Fear at 16/100. Bitcoin has historically produced its strongest returns when purchased during extreme fear. Every previous extreme fear event resolved to new all-time highs.
Network hash rate at 1015.5 EH/s. Miners invest long-term capital in hardware and electricity. High hash rate signals sustained miner confidence in Bitcoin's future value.
Transaction fee to confirm fast: 1 sat/vB. Low fees present opportunity for UTXO consolidation and coin movement. Lightning Network bypasses the fee market entirely.
Bitcoin Stock-to-Flow ratio: 121.9. S2F model price: $733,813. Bitcoin trading BELOW model — historically bullish signal.
NVT Signal: 48.53 (undervalued). NVT compares network value to economic throughput — Bitcoin's P/E ratio. Undervalued relative to economic activity.
The Bitcoin halving is the most predictable supply shock in financial history. Every 210,000 blocks new supply is cut in half — mathematically enforced, immune to political interference.

todays_tips.tip: Use a hardware wallet for any Bitcoin you are not actively transacting — cold storage is the gold standard.
Dollar-cost averaging (DCA) removes the need to time the market. Consistent accumulation beats speculation.
Run your own full node to validate transactions without trusting anyone — sovereignty means verification.
Never share your seed phrase with anyone, ever. Not exchanges, not support, not anyone.
Fear and Greed at extreme fear — historically the best long-term accumulation periods.

intelligence.todays_fact: Running your own full node means you trust no one — you verify everything yourself.

headlines.title: Bitcoin miners face a tougher road to the 2028 halving
headlines.summary: Bitcoin miners are heading toward the 2028 halving with thinner margins, tighter power markets and a growing need for capital discipline.

headlines.title: Bitcoin hits $73K as cool US CPI data shows 60-year record gas price hike
headlines.summary: Bitcoin saw a fresh attempt to hit new local highs on the back of lower-than-expected US CPI data, despite a giant gas-price increase.

headlines.title: Bitcoin analysis sees $55K BTC price 'iron bottom' by December 2026
headlines.summary: New BTC price analysis predicted that the bear market would bottom out later in the year, before beginning a "two-year accumulation phase."

headlines.title: The Core Issue: The Role and History of Bitcoin Core Maintainers
headlines.summary:

The Core Issue: The Role and History of Bitcoin Core Maintainers
Bitcoin Core’s maintainer system began with Satoshi Nakamoto merging every commit himself. Today, trusted key holders

headlines.title: Strategy's Michael Saylor signals impending Bitcoin purchase
headlines.summary: The company has completed 105 Bitcoin transactions since 2020 and is playing contrarian as it continues accumulating BTC via corporate debt and equity financing

headlines.title: DeFi’s shakeout is a stress test, not a death sentence
headlines.summary: Novozhenov argues that despite lingering governance, security and regulatory hurdles that have shuttered several protocols, DeFi remains resilient.

headlines.title: Bitcoin may be forming a base at $65,000 as 'paper hands' have been flushed out
headlines.summary: Jurrien Timmer, director of global macro at Fidelity Investments, says strong earnings are helping markets absorb geopolitical shocks, despite ongoing risks.

headlines.title: There’s a Way to Make Bitcoin Safe From Quantum Without a Fork, Researchers Say
headlines.summary: A new proposal suggests Bitcoin users could defend against future quantum attacks using a transaction design that works within the network’s existing rules.

headlines.title: 'Not Going to Stop at Bitcoin': Morgan Stanley Weighs Tokenization, Tax Solutions in Crypto Push
headlines.summary: Morgan Stanley’s Amy Oldenburg signaled that the Wall Street giant’s crypto journey has a long way to go.

headlines.title: Aria Token Rebounds From 80% Crash to Hit New All-Time High of $0.95

headlines.title: Bitcoin price analysis sees new short squeeze as open interest nears $25B
headlines.summary: Bitcoin open interest hit five-week highs while funding rates mimicked the BTC price collapse below $60,000, leading analysis to predict a new short squeeze.

headlines.title: Crypto yields are falling below TradFi
headlines.summary:
Crypto investors used to receive compensation for taking outsized risk depositing assets into DeFi, but many yields have fallen below TradFi.

headlines.title: Mempool Space Invaders will give you $7 if you move 10,000 BTC
headlines.summary:
The quirky BTC-themed Space Invaders clone is offering 10,000 Satoshis (worth $7) to whoever can get 10,000 points. It ain't easy.

headlines.title: Justin Sun calls out WLFI, platform threatens lawsuit in response
headlines.summary: Sun criticized the WLFI platform over long token lockup periods and accused it of having blacklist functions at the smart contract level.

headlines.title: CFTC Chair Mike Selig argues for agency's 'exclusive regulatory authority' in prediction markets fight: State of Crypto
headlines.summary: Selig said states do not have the ability to police prediction market providers, echoing his agency's stance as it pursues court cases to cement its authority.

headlines.title: Want Claude Opus AI on Your Potato PC? This Is Your Next-Best Bet
headlines.summary: A developer distilled Claude Opus 4.6's reasoning into a local Qwen model anyone can run. The result is Qwopus—and it's surprisingly close to the real thing.

headlines.title: ‘See you in court’: WLFI threatens Justin Sun after he alleges hidden blacklist backdoor in token contract
headlines.summary: Sun called himself "the first and single largest victim" of World Liberty's blacklist, referencing his own wallet which has been frozen since Sep. 2025.

questions.question: What does 'not your keys not your coins' mean?
options: If you don't control private keys you don't truly own it

questions.question: What year was the Bitcoin whitepaper published?
What is the NVT ratio?
options: Comparing market cap to on-chain tx volume (Bitcoin P/E ratio)

questions.question: How often does the Bitcoin halving occur?
questions.explanation: Every 210,000 blocks the block reward is cut in half.

questions.question: What is the smallest unit of Bitcoin?

terms.HODL: Hold On for Dear Life. Originally a typo. Now a philosophy: don't trade, just hold through cycles.
terms.Satoshi: The smallest unit of Bitcoin: 0.00000001 BTC. Named after Bitcoin's anonymous creator, Satoshi Nakamoto.
terms.UTXO: Unspent Transaction Output. Bitcoin doesn't have accounts — it has UTXOs. Each transaction consumes old UTXOs and creates new ones.
terms.Halving: Every 210,000 blocks (~4 years), the block reward is cut in half. Reduces new Bitcoin supply permanently. Precedes every major bull run.
terms.Mempool: Memory pool of unconfirmed transactions waiting to be included in a block. High mempool = high fees = high demand.
terms.Lightning Network: Bitcoin's Layer 2. Enables instant, near-free payments via payment channels. Scales Bitcoin to billions of users.
terms.Hash Rate: The total computational power securing the Bitcoin network. Higher hash rate = more secure, more decentralized, more committed miners.
terms.Block: Bundle of Bitcoin transactions permanently recorded on the blockchain every ~10 minutes.
terms.Mining: Securing Bitcoin by solving SHA-256 proof-of-work puzzles to add blocks.
terms.SegWit: Segregated Witness (2017). Separates signature data. Enables Lightning. Fixes transaction malleability.
terms.Taproot: Bitcoin upgrade (Nov 2021). Adds Schnorr signatures, MAST, and Tapscript. More private, flexible, and efficient transactions.
terms.Full Node: A computer running Bitcoin Core that independently validates every transaction and block. The backbone of decentralization.
terms.Private Key: 256-bit secret number proving ownership and signing Bitcoin transactions.
terms.BIP: Bitcoin Improvement Proposal. How protocol changes are proposed, discussed, and implemented. Governance through code.
terms.CoinJoin: Privacy technique combining multiple transactions into one. Breaks chain analysis. Implemented in Wasabi, JoinMarket.
terms.S2F: Stock-to-Flow — ratio of existing supply to annual new production. Higher = more scarce.
terms.NVT: Network Value to Transactions — Bitcoin's P/E ratio equivalent.
terms.Difficulty Adjustment: Every 2016 blocks, Bitcoin automatically adjusts mining difficulty to maintain ~10 minute block times. Self-regulating system.
terms.Genesis Block: Block 0. Mined by Satoshi on January 3, 2009. Contains message: 'Chancellor on brink of second bailout for banks.'
terms.Multisig: Multi-signature — requires M-of-N private keys to authorize a transaction.
terms.P2P: Peer-to-Peer — direct transactions between parties without intermediaries.
terms.Proof of Work: Bitcoin's consensus mechanism. Miners compete to find a valid hash. Energy expenditure = unforgeable proof of commitment.
terms.Hard Fork: Incompatible protocol change creating a separate blockchain.
terms.Script: Bitcoin's simple, intentionally non-Turing-complete programming language. Governs spending conditions for all UTXOs.
terms.HTLC: Hash Time-Locked Contract — cryptographic mechanism enabling Lightning payments.
terms.Schnorr: Signature scheme in Taproot enabling key aggregation and better privacy.
terms.Ordinals: Protocol for inscribing data onto individual satoshis.
terms.Seed Phrase: 12 or 24 words that recover all private keys. The master key to your Bitcoin. Never digital, never photographed.
terms.Layer 2: Protocols built on Bitcoin (like Lightning) for scalability.
terms.21 million: The absolute hard cap on Bitcoin supply. Enforced by every full node. Cannot be changed without unanimous consensus.
terms.Fear & Greed Index: 0-100 sentiment indicator. 0 = extreme fear (buy signal historically). 100 = extreme greed (caution). Contrarian tool.
terms.MVRV Ratio: Market Value to Realized Value. Below 1 = historically excellent buy zone. Above 3.5 = historically overvalued zone.
terms.Stock-to-Flow: Ratio of existing supply to new supply. Bitcoin's S2F increases with each halving, making it scarcer than gold.
terms.Multi-sig: Transactions requiring multiple private keys to sign. M-of-N security. Gold standard for significant holdings.
terms.Schnorr Signatures: More efficient than ECDSA. Enables key aggregation (multiple signers = one signature). Foundation of Taproot.
terms.Byzantine Generals Problem: Classic distributed computing problem Satoshi solved: how to reach consensus without trusting any party.
terms.Realized Price: Average price at which all Bitcoin last moved. Below realized price = underwater holders = capitulation zone.
terms.NVT Signal: Network Value to Transactions. High NVT = overvalued relative to economic activity. Low NVT = undervalued.
terms.Timelock: Bitcoin script feature that locks funds until a future block height or timestamp. Foundation of Lightning channels.
terms.Sats: Short for satoshis. 100 million sats = 1 BTC. The future unit of account for a Bitcoin-standard world.
terms.Block Subsidy: Newly created Bitcoin paid to miners per block. Currently 3.125 BTC. Decreases each halving until ~2140.
terms.Timechain: Satoshi's original name for what we call blockchain. A chain of time-stamped cryptographic commitments.

beginner.title: What is Bitcoin?
beginner.lesson: Bitcoin is digital money that no government, bank, or corporation controls. Only 21 million will ever exist. No one can shut it down, freeze your funds, or inflate the supply.

beginner.title: Why Bitcoin?
beginner.lesson: Bitcoin solves the double-spend problem without a trusted third party. It is the first scarce digital asset with mathematically enforced monetary policy.

beginner.title: How to Get Bitcoin
beginner.lesson: Buy on regulated exchanges (Coinbase, Kraken, Strike), earn it for services, or mine it. Always withdraw to self-custody — not your keys, not your coins.

beginner.title: Sending and Receiving
beginner.lesson: Bitcoin transactions are sent to addresses derived from public keys. Transactions confirm in ~10 minutes per block. 6 confirmations is essentially irreversible.

intermediate.title: The Halving Cycle
intermediate.lesson: Every 210,000 blocks the block reward halves. This programmatic supply reduction has historically preceded major bull markets.

intermediate.title: Lightning Network Deep Dive
intermediate.lesson: Lightning opens payment channels funded with Bitcoin. Payments route through the network instantly for near-zero fees. HTLCs enforce trustless routing.

intermediate.title: Understanding UTXOs
intermediate.lesson: Bitcoin tracks ownership through Unspent Transaction Outputs, not account balances. Each UTXO is locked to a public key. Spending creates new UTXOs.

intermediate.title: Mempool and Fee Markets
intermediate.lesson: Unconfirmed transactions sit in the mempool competing by fee rate (sat/vB). Miners prioritize higher fees. Lightning bypasses this entirely.

advanced.title: Taproot and Schnorr
advanced.lesson: Taproot (BIP 340-342) activated November 2021. Schnorr signatures enable key aggregation (MuSig). MAST hides unused script branches. Major privacy and efficiency improvement.

advanced.title: Stock-to-Flow Model
advanced.lesson: S2F = existing supply / annual new production. Bitcoin post-2024 halving S2F is ~121, surpassing gold. The model predicts price based on scarcity ratio.

advanced.title: On-Chain Analytics
advanced.lesson: NVT, MVRV, SOPR, and Puell Multiple are key on-chain metrics for valuation and cycle analysis. Each measures a different aspect of Bitcoin network economics.

advanced.title: Bitcoin Privacy
advanced.lesson: Bitcoin is pseudonymous not anonymous. CoinJoin combines transactions to break chain analysis. Taproot improves privacy. PayNyms (BIP47) enable stealth addresses.

all_facts: Bitcoin's genesis block contains 'Chancellor on brink of second bailout for banks' — a permanent statement etched forever.
Only 21 million Bitcoin will ever exist. ~3-4 million are already permanently lost.
Satoshi mined roughly 1 million BTC and has never moved a single satoshi.
Bitcoin halvings occur every 210,000 blocks (~4 years), cutting new supply in half.
The Lightning Network can process millions of transactions per second.
Bitcoin's hash rate would take all Earth's supercomputers billions of years to crack one private key.
One satoshi = 0.00000001 BTC. Bitcoin is divisible to 8 decimal places — 2.1 quadrillion satoshis total.
Bitcoin has had 100% uptime since January 3, 2009.
El Salvador was the first nation to make Bitcoin legal tender in 2021.
The first real-world Bitcoin transaction: 10,000 BTC for two Papa John's pizzas (May 22, 2010).
Bitcoin's difficulty adjusts every 2,016 blocks to keep block times at ~10 minutes.
Taproot (BIP 340-342) activated at block 709,632 in November 2021.
The block subsidy reaches zero around year 2140 — then miners earn only from fees.
SegWit (BIP141) fixed transaction malleability in 2017.
A Bitcoin private key is a 256-bit number — 2^256 possible keys, more than atoms in the universe.
Bitcoin's monetary policy is set in stone — no one can change the 21M cap.
The P2P network has thousands of nodes across 100+ countries.
CoinJoin and PayJoin are privacy techniques that obscure Bitcoin transaction trails.
Bitcoin's S2F ratio surpassed gold's after the 2020 halving.
Running your own full node means you trust no one — you verify everything yourself.
